data.title: Parsons gets $25M Middle-East transportation contracts
data.description:
• Parsons (NYSE:PSN) awarded two contracts worth $25M to perform design engineering for a Middle East transportation customer.
  • With the same customer, Parsons (PSN) has won more than $90M in contract awards over the last 9 months.
  • Under these contracts, Parsons (PSN) will lead the design engineering and construction supervision of transportation infrastructure enhancements.
  • These wins are part of a broader strategic focus of the Gulf member states including Bahrain, Qatar, Abu Dhabi, Saudi Vision 2030, Oman Vision 2040, and Dubai 2040 Urban Master Plan.

data.title: First Horizon National Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42 beats by $0.04, revenue of $828M beats by $5.64M
data.description:
• First Horizon National press release (NYSE:FHN): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $828M (+6.4% Y/Y) beats by $5.64M.

data.title: Matador Resources lifts dividend by 25% to $0.25/share
data.description:
• Matador Resources (NYSE:MTDR) declares $0.25/share quarterly dividend, 25% increase from prior dividend of $0.20.
  • Forward yield 1.97%
  • Payable Dec. 6; for shareholders of record Nov. 15; ex-div Nov. 15.
  • See MTDR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: adidas shows better-than-expected Q3 numbers, raises guidance
data.description:
adidas (OTCQX:ADDYY) (OTCQX:ADDDF) released its preliminary results for Q3, showcasing a strong performance that exceeded market expectations and brand momentum for higher estimates for sales and profit in 2024.

The German sports company (OTCQX:ADDYY) raised its full-year guidance, anticipates a 10% increase in currency-neutral revenues for 2024, compared to its earlier high-single-digit prediction. Operating profit is projected at approximately €1.2 billion, up from the previous estimate of €1.0 billion.

Q3 currency-neutral revenues increased 10% versus the prior year. In Euro terms, Q3 revenues grew 7% to €6.438 billion. The gross margin improved to 51.3% from 49.3% the previous year.

Besides, operating profit for Q3 increased to €598 million, up from €409 million in 2023, which includes a contribution of approximately €50 million from the sale of remaining Yeezy inventory.

adidas (OTCQX:ADDYY) stock price traded ~3% lower on the European stock exchange.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Synchrony Financial beats Q3 estimates, updates FY24 EPS view
VSE Corporation prices $142M stock offering at $87 per share
data.description:
• VSE Corporation (NASDAQ:VSEC) priced its previously announced underwritten public offering.
  • The company is offering 1,724,137 shares at a price to the public of $87.00 per share.
  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 258,620 shares.
  • The offering is expected to close on October 17, 2024.
  • Net proceeds are expected to be ~$142.5M and a portion of it will be used to finance a portion of the cash consideration for its previously announced acquisition of Kellstrom Aerospace Group.
  • The stock price traded 1.1% lower on Wednesday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: O3 Mining to conduct normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• O3 Mining (OTCQX:OIIIF) said the TSX Venture Exchange has accepted the firm's notice to implement a normal course issuer bid.
  • The normal course issuer bid is to purchase, for cancellation, up to 8.25M shares.

data.title: Synchrony Financial GAAP EPS of $1.94 beats by $0.14, revenue of $4.61B beats by $860M
data.description:
• Synchrony Financial press release (NYSE:SYF): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.94 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $4.61B (+5.7% Y/Y) beats by $860M.
  • Purchase volume decreased 4% to $45.0 billion• Loan receivables increased 4% to $102.2 billion• Average active accounts remained flat at 70.4 million• New accounts decreased 18% to 4.7 million• Net interest margin decreased 32 basis points to 15.04%• Efficiency ratio decreased 200 basis points to 31.2%

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures tread cautiously after ASML's weak outlook
data.description:
Stock index futures traded cautiously, as traders assessed ASML's weak net sales guidance for 2025, which also pulled down chip stocks.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.2% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.01%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points to 3.93%.

Wall Street closed in the red on Tuesday, as chip stocks took it on the chin following disappointing earnings from Dutch giant ASML (ASML) and news that the U.S. was mulling further export caps.

"Some rare weakness has come through in risk over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 falling back from its record high the previous day," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

ASML (ASML) shares were down 4% in premarket trade, after the firm said it expects 2025 net sales forecast to be between €30B to €35B (mid-point of €32.5B). The Dutch company also missed its third-quarter bookings estimates.

On the economic side, the September import/export prices and treasury buyback results are expected. The export prices index is expected to come in at -0.4%, while the import at -0.3% on a monthly basis.
